subject: Physics
title: Einstein's Elevator
description: A micro-lesson that makes Einstein's Elevator usable.

hook.text: Einstein imagined a person in a sealed elevator. They couldn't tell if they were on Earth or accelerating through space.

buildup.text: Standing in a stationary elevator on Earth, you feel weight from gravity pulling you down.

discovery.text: In space, an elevator accelerating upward at 9.8 m/s² produces the exact same sensation. Gravity and acceleration feel identical.

twist.text: This equivalence principle became the foundation of general relativity—gravity isn't a force, it's curved spacetime.

climax.text: Einstein called it 'the happiest thought of my life.' It led to his greatest theory.

punchline.text: Gravity feels like acceleration. Einstein's happiest thought reshaped physics.

quiz.question: What is the equivalence principle?
options: Gravity and acceleration produce identical physical effects
All elevators experience the same gravity
